title: carex hovarum
intro: Carex hovarum, also known as Hovar's sedge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in wetlands and marshes. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carex hovarum include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reduce inflammation, and promote digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested for its rhizomes, which are dried and then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
